title: Create initial error types and config loading foundation
description: Implement the core error enum DaemonError and the TOML config parsing/validation layer needed before other modules can be built. Deliverables: src/errors.rs with JSON-RPC error-code mapping, src/config.rs with DaemonConfig/BotConfig/SigningConfig structs, env-var and path expansion, duplicate bot_id validation, config file permission checks, and unit tests. Covers R7, R8, and U1.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes for config and error modules; invalid config cases (duplicate bot_id, loose permissions, missing fields) return clear errors

title: Set up schema-first contract artifacts and codegen
description: Establish the schemas/ directory as the canonical machine-readable contract and wire the cargo xtask codegen runner. Deliverables: schemas/config.json, schemas/jsonrpc.json, schemas/metrics.json, schemas/service-compatibility.json, xtask/src/main.rs with a codegen command, generated Rust type stubs, and tests/schema_sync.rs that fails when schemas and generated code diverge. This satisfies R32 and U13.
acceptance_criteria: schemas/ contains four JSON files; cargo xtask codegen runs and writes generated types; cargo test includes schema_sync.rs

title: Initialize and scaffold pacto-bot-api Rust project
description: Create the foundational Rust crate structure for the pacto-bot-api daemon. This is greenfield work: no src/, Cargo.toml, or build files exist yet. Deliverables: Cargo.toml with binary targets (pacto-bot-api and pacto-bot-admin), src/ module tree (main.rs, admin.rs, config.rs, errors.rs, etc.), basic CLI argument parsing with clap, initial TOML config loader with serde, example config file, and a passing cargo check. This unblocks all subsequent implementation units U1–U15.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check passes; both binary targets resolve; src/ module tree matches plan section 'Crate Structure'; example config exists and parses
notes: Reopened for validation of generated scaffold files
close_reason: Validated: cargo check, clippy, test pass; both binaries build; src/, tests/, schemas/, xtask/ layout matches plan; CLI help works

title: Implement admin CLI: state migration, validation, and token rotation
description: Implement export <bot_id> (JSON state dump with metadata and split-brain warning, no nsec), import <bot_id> <state.json>, validate-config, and rotate-http-token. Refuse export/import if daemon.lock is held. Requirements: R10, R11, R25, R29. Implementation Unit: U12c.
acceptance_criteria: Export/import round-trip works; lock-held refused; validate-config reports issues; rotation atomically rewrites token.

title: Implement admin CLI: bunker testing
description: Implement pacto-bot-admin test-bunker <bot_id> to read config, connect to the bot's bunker, request get_public_key, and verify the pubkey matches the configured npub. Exit 0 on match, non-zero on mismatch or unreachable bunker. Requirements: R6. Implementation Unit: U12b.
acceptance_criteria: Valid bunker exits 0; pubkey mismatch exits non-zero; unreachable bunker exits non-zero.

title: Add Rust agent skills for Claude, Cursor, and OMP
description: Install Rust-focused agent skills (rust-best-practices, rust-async-patterns, rust-testing, rust-patterns, m15-anti-pattern, cargo-fuzz, cargo-nextest, ce-compound, ce-compound-refresh) from skills.sh for Claude Code, Cursor, and Oh My Pi. Vendor them into .claude/skills, .agents/skills, and .omp/skills. Update AGENTS.md with an Agent Skills section and DEVELOPMENT.md with project-specific guidance (cargo nextest, no pacto-dev-env references).
acceptance_criteria: Skills installed and copied to .claude/skills, .agents/skills, .omp/skills; skills-lock.json created; AGENTS.md lists skills; DEVELOPMENT.md is accurate for pacto-bot-api; changes committed to add-rust-agent-skills branch
close_reason: Installed skills, updated docs, committed and pushed to add-rust-agent-skills branch

title: Document project overview and developer onboarding
description: Create README.md, DEVELOPMENT.md, and supporting docs that explain the 5 Ws (who, what, when, where, why), how to use the daemon and admin CLI, and how to set up a development environment. These files replace ad-hoc onboarding with a single source of truth for operators and contributors.
acceptance_criteria: README.md and DEVELOPMENT.md exist and cover: project purpose and 5 Ws, quickstart for running daemon and admin CLI, config file explanation with security warnings, development setup (Rust toolchain, cargo commands, test modes), and links to schemas/ and plan docs
close_reason: README.md and DEVELOPMENT.md created, verified cargo check and cargo test pass

title: Configure Rust linting and security tooling
description: Add linting and audit configuration that enforces the security posture from the plan, especially secret hygiene. Deliverables: clippy.toml with custom lints forbidding plain String for secrets, deny.toml for cargo-deny audit gates, and initial CI-friendly verification commands. This supports R34 and U15.
acceptance_criteria: cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes on scaffolded code; cargo deny check runs without configuration errors
